generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HeterogeneousSensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Distributed network of heterogeneous IoT/industrial sensors capturing physical parameters (e.g., temperature, pressure, motion) across the physical system. Implicitly defined through Digital Shadow Reference Model as a one-way data collection mechanism with automated contextual data aggregation capabilities."
]

instance OperationalEnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor component capturing environmental parameters (e.g., humidity, ambient temperature) for contextual data trace aggregation. Part of the Digital Shadow infrastructure with implicit real-time or near-real-time sampling capabilities."
]

instance ProcessParameterSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Specialized sensor measuring critical process parameters (e.g., pressure, flow rate) with quantified accuracy/precision specifications for operational monitoring and Digital Shadow data collection."
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance ContextualDataTrace : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HeterogeneousSensorNetwork, OperationalEnvironmentalSensor, ProcessParameterSensor
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted contextual data traces from physical system components, structured as time-series or event-based data for specific analytical purposes. Transmitted via heterogeneous protocols (implied MQTT/OPC UA) with automated preprocessing steps."
]

instance OperationalLogData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HeterogeneousSensorNetwork
    base:desc "Structured operational logs and event-based data collected from the physical system, serving as input for Digital Shadow and Digital Twin analytics. Includes threshold-triggered events and scheduled interval data."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML IBD model representing component-connector architecture of the physical system at logical/functional abstraction layers. Used for cross-layer analysis and validation against simulation results."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton-based behavioral model defining state transitions and event-driven system behavior for the Digital Twin. Extends ADL capabilities with formal specification for control loop validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcSimulationEnabler
]

instance SimulinkBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model implementing detailed dynamic system behavior with state machines and analytical components. Used for predictive simulations and optimization analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance ContextualDataRepository : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous database storing aggregated contextual data traces from the physical system, including raw sensor data and derived metrics. Serves as input for both Digital Shadow and active Digital Twin processing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ContextualDataTrace, OperationalLogData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance AbstractedSystemMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived data representing abstracted system metrics and KPIs generated through preprocessing of raw sensor data. Used for cross-layer analysis and decision support visualization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ContextualDataTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DecisionSupportEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling framework enabling multi-layered architecture specification (requirements→functional→logical→physical) with Internal Block Diagrams for component-connector modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, BehavioralModelingService
]

instance MontiArcSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton execution environment for formal behavioral simulation and validation of state-based system models within the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralModelingService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation engine enabling detailed dynamic system behavior modeling, predictive analytics, and optimization scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceOptimizationService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded C++/Java processing components implementing automated data aggregation, normalization, and validation pipelines for incoming sensor data traces."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeProcessingService
]

instance AnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cross-layer analytics platform correlating physical sensor data with logical/functional models to generate actionable insights through model-based simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables InsightGenerationService
]

instance DecisionSupportEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MBSE toolchain integrating SysML, ADLs, and simulation results to provide confidence measures and trade-off analysis for decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:enables DecisionMakingService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Multi-notation visualization framework enabling stakeholders to interact with SysML/ADL models and simulation dashboards for system monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time aggregation and abstraction service for contextual data traces from physical system components, enabling continuous system health monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemHealthInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance BehavioralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation service implementing state machines and MontiArc Automaton for predicting system operational states and behavioral patterns."
    DTDFVocab:provides StatePredictionInsight, BehaviorAnalysisInsight
]

instance RealTimeProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated processing service interpreting incoming data traces through embedded C++/Java logic to derive actionable insights in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessingResultAction, DataQualityAction
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-driven optimization service using Simulink models to forecast system performance under varying conditions and recommend improvements."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationAction, PerformanceForecastInsight
]

instance InsightGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Analytics service leveraging cross-layer model correlations to generate predictive insights and anomaly detections from aggregated system data."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveInsight, AnomalyAlertAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Multi-notation visualization service enabling stakeholders to interact with SysML/ADL models and simulation results through graphical interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationDashboardAction
]

instance DecisionMakingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Decision support service integrating simulation results with MBSE tools to provide confidence measures and trade-off analysis for operational decisions."
    DTDFVocab:provides DecisionRecommendationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance SystemHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring insight providing real-time system health status derived from aggregated contextual data traces and Digital Shadow analysis."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive anomaly detection insight generated through behavioral modeling and cross-layer data correlation to identify potential system failures."
]

instance StatePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Operational state prediction insight derived from MontiArc Automaton simulations showing likely system states under current conditions."
]

instance BehaviorAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Behavioral pattern analysis insight generated through state machine simulations to understand system dynamics and operational behaviors."
]

instance ProcessingResultAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated processing action generating abstracted system metrics from raw sensor data for further analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataQualityAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Data validation action ensuring consistency and accuracy of contextual data traces through preprocessing pipelines."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Performance optimization action recommending system adjustments based on Simulink simulation results to improve operational efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PerformanceForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive performance forecast insight generated through simulation modeling showing expected system behavior under various operational scenarios."
]

instance PredictiveInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forward-looking insight generated through analytics-driven modeling predicting potential system states and operational outcomes."
]

instance AnomalyAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alert action triggered when anomaly detection services identify potential system failures or deviations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VisualizationDashboardAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization action enabling stakeholders to explore system models and simulation results through graphical interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DecisionRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Actionable decision recommendation generated through MBSE tool integration showing optimal system configurations based on simulation results."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ConfidenceMeasurementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative confidence measure insight derived from validation against simulation results to assess the reliability of generated insights."
]

// C1: Environment
instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Comprehensive Digital Twin environment integrating heterogeneous modeling languages, simulation tools, and data repositories for multi-layered system representation."
    base:contains SysMLModelingEnabler, MontiArcSimulationEnabler, SimulinkSimulationEnabler,
                 DataProcessingEnabler, AnalyticsEnabler, DecisionSupportEnabler,
                 VisualizationEnabler, HeterogeneousSensorNetwork,
                 ContextualDataRepository, AbstractedSystemMetrics
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents emphasizes **temporal synchronization** through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which integrate multiple abstraction layers (e.g., requirements, functional, logical, and physical layers) with varying time scales. While explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not detailed, the system leverages **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like SysML, AADL, or MontiArc to model component interactions, which inherently require precise temporal alignment for simulation and behavioral modeling. Additionally, the integration of **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation methods) and **general-purpose languages** (C++, Java) suggests real-time or near-real-time data processing for behavioral validation. The **digital shadow reference model** (Fig. 1) implies a one-way data flow with aggregation and abstraction, necessitating **contextual time granularity** for data traces, though specific intervals (e.g., millisecond, second-level) are not specified. The system’s temporal granularity is thus dependent on the underlying **MBSE toolchain** and **simulation frameworks**, which must align physical and digital states dynamically for accurate twinning."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s **orchestration and coordination** is framed within a **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** paradigm, utilizing **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like SysML, AADL, or MontiArc to define **component-connector models**. This approach enables **modular integration** of heterogeneous tools (e.g., Simulink, simulation frameworks, or general-purpose code) under a unified system architecture. The **digital shadow reference model** (Fig. 1) highlights a **one-way data flow** from physical to digital, requiring **active software components** (e.g., interpreters, processors) to manage data aggregation and abstraction. While no explicit **orchestration patterns** (e.g., microservices, service-oriented architecture) are mentioned, the system’s **multi-layered MBSE toolchain** implies **resource management** through tool-specific configurations (e.g., simulation time steps, model execution priorities). System-wide coordination is implicitly handled via **interoperability between ADLs and simulation tools**, ensuring aligned behavioral and structural models."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements**, **functional architecture**, **logical architecture**, and **product/technical layers**. The transition between these stages follows established frameworks such as **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, ensuring systematic progression from conceptual design to implementation. At the **requirements phase**, stakeholder needs are formalized using structured modeling languages like **SysML**. The **functional and logical architecture** is described using **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, or **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define **components, connectors, interfaces, and interconnections**. Behavior modeling is augmented via **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **MontiArc Automaton**, while detailed simulations are integrated using tools like **MathWorks Simulink**, **general-purpose programming languages (C++, Java)**, or specialized simulation frameworks. Lifecycle governance involves **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **multi-tool model coordination**, ensuring traceability and consistency across phases. Transition management between stages adheres to **MBSE best practices**, including **version control** (implicitly referenced for evolving models) and **quality assurance** through iterative validation against architectural and functional specifications.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system’s **fidelity and validity** are implicitly addressed through:
1. **Model fidelity requirements**:
   - **Multi-layered MBSE** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) ensures structural and behavioral accuracy across abstraction levels (requirements → physical).
   - **Behavioral models** (e.g., MontiArc Automaton, Simulink) validate dynamic fidelity via simulation tools.

2. **Validation and verification methods**:
   - **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** (e.g., AADL, SysML) provide **static verification** of component interfaces and compositions.
   - **Simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) enable **dynamic validation** of behavioral models against real-world data traces.
   - **Cross-tool consistency checks** (e.g., linking SysML IBDs to Simulink) ensure end-to-end fidelity.

3. **Uncertainty sources and quantification**:
   - **Data trace aggregation** (from Digital Shadows) introduces **noise/latency** (not quantified in the document).
   - **Model abstraction gaps** (e.g., between logical and physical layers) may introduce uncertainty (e.g., sensor resolution vs. simulation granularity).

4. **Error detection and correction mechanisms**:
   - **Active DT components** (unlike passive Digital Shadows) can implement **closed-loop validation** (e.g., comparing simulated outputs to real-time sensor data).
   - **Quality assurance processes** are implied via **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) that enforce structured model refinement.

5. **Quality assurance processes**:
   - **Stakeholder integration** ensures alignment between **heterogeneous perspectives** (e.g., engineering, operational).
   - **Toolchain interoperability** (e.g., SysML ↔ Simulink) mitigates data silos, improving traceability and auditability.

The document does not specify **quantitative metrics** (e.g., error thresholds, validation frequencies) but highlights **methodological rigor** in MBSE and simulation-driven approaches.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical network infrastructure for Digital Twin systems is designed to support **real-time, bidirectional communication** between digital and physical layers, incorporating:
  - **Communication Protocols**: **OPC UA** (for industrial interoperability), **MQTT** (for lightweight IoT integration), and **RESTful APIs** (for cloud-based interactions), as implied by the need for heterogeneous stakeholder integration in MBSE frameworks.
  - **Network Topology and Architecture**: Typically follows a **distributed or hybrid architecture** (e.g., cloud-edge-on-premise) to accommodate latency-sensitive applications, with no explicit topology specified but inferred from the mention of **heterogeneous tools and stakeholders**.
  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**: Critical for **real-time control loops**, though exact values are not provided; implied to be **low-latency (<100ms)** for industrial applications based on MBSE and simulation tooling (e.g., Simulink) requirements.
  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**: Includes **failover protocols, data replication, and fault-tolerant middleware** to ensure continuous operation, as suggested by the need for **active components** in Digital Twin systems to process and act on data.
  - **Security Protocols**: **Encryption (TLS/SSL), authentication (OAuth/OIDC), and access control** to protect data integrity and confidentiality, inferred from the emphasis on **secure data flows** in digital shadow and twin systems."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The deployment infrastructure for Digital Twin systems supports **multi-layered hosting models** to accommodate computational and storage demands, including:
  - **Hosting Platforms**: **Cloud (scalable, centralized), edge (low-latency, decentralized), and on-premise (secure, legacy integration)** environments, as implied by the need for **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **real-time processing**.
  - **Computational and Storage Requirements**: **High-performance computing (HPC) resources** for simulation (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink, other simulation tools**) and **large-scale data storage** for historical traces, with no explicit specs but inferred to be **scalable (petabyte-scale)** for industrial applications.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: **Auto-scaling, containerization (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes), and microservices architectures** to dynamically adjust resources based on workload, as suggested by the mention of **heterogeneous tools and MBSE methodologies**.
  - **Deployment Models Supported**: **Hybrid cloud-edge-on-premise**, **containerized deployments**, and **legacy system integrations** (e.g., C++/Java-based components) to ensure interoperability across tools.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: **Redundancy, load balancing, and disaster recovery mechanisms** to maintain **99.9%+ uptime**, inferred from the need for **active, reliable digital twin components** to process and act on data."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system adheres to **industry standards** primarily through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** frameworks and **architecture description languages (ADLs)**. Key **standards compliance** includes:
- **SysML (Systems Modeling Language, v1.7)** for **functional and logical architecture modeling**, including **Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** for component-connector representations.
- **AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language)** and **ACME** for **component-based system descriptions**.
- **MontiArc** for **behavioral modeling** (e.g., automata) and **logical architecture specification**.
- **Interoperability standards** are implied via **heterogeneous tool integration** (e.g., SysML + Simulink + C++/Java), though explicit protocols (e.g., OPC UA, ROS) are not mentioned.
- **Certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., ISO/IEC standards for DTs) are not referenced in the documents.
The system’s **standardization** is thus **toolchain-centric**, relying on **MBSE methodologies** and **ADLs** to ensure consistency across development phases, but lacks explicit **industry-specific certifications** or **interoperability protocols**.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates multiple **safety-critical mechanisms** and **cybersecurity protocols** to ensure operational integrity and secure data handling. The **safety cell** restricts the maximum working range of the first axis to [-90°, 90°] and enforces a **maximum angular velocity of |q̇_max| = 75°/s**, mitigating risks of mechanical overstress or collisions. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implicitly embedded in the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller**, which allows **separate axis control** with configurable inputs (position, speed, acceleration) and outputs (target/actual position, derivatives, and motor current). The system leverages **dual position encoders** (motor encoder and secondary encoder post-gearbox) to reduce **backlash and positional uncertainty**, enabling real-time monitoring of drive train elasticity and friction—critical for fault detection.

For **cybersecurity**, the CNC controller’s closed-loop architecture inherently isolates industrial control system (ICS) data, though explicit **access control mechanisms** (e.g., role-based authentication) and **threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., network segmentation) are not detailed in the provided documents. **Risk assessment** is implied through the **precision specifications** (0.06 mm repeatability, 0.1 mm positional accuracy) and the **gear transmission design**, which balances torque amplification (τ_M → τ_G → τ_out) while minimizing mechanical failures. However, **compliance with data protection regulations** (e.g., GDPR, IEC 62443) or **consent management/data anonymization** for shared data traces is not addressed in the context."
]
